description:
You are an edge–computing engineer preparing the next patch-release of the firmware-deployment helper that runs on IoT gateways.
    A small Git repository already lives in /home/user/edge-devices.  
    Your job is to perform a standard semantic-version bump (patch bump), update the changelog, generate a release log, and create a signed Git tag for the new release.

    CURRENT STATE
    1. A Git repository exists at /home/user/edge-devices.  
    2. Important files inside that repository:
       • /home/user/edge-devices/VERSION  
         └── contains one line only: 0.9.4  
       • /home/user/edge-devices/CHANGELOG.md  
         └── already holds earlier release notes and ends with the section  
            “## [0.9.4] – 2023-10-07”.  
       • /home/user/edge-devices/src/main.py – not relevant for this task.
    3. There is **no** Git tag for 0.9.5 yet.
    4. The default branch is “main”.

    WHAT YOU MUST DO
    Perform the following sequence (5-10 shell commands in total is normal; exact commands are up to you):

    Step 1 – Version bump  
       • Change the single line in /home/user/edge-devices/VERSION from  
         0.9.4 to 0.9.5

    Step 2 – Changelog entry  
       • Pre-pend a new section to /home/user/edge-devices/CHANGELOG.md that exactly matches the template below.  
       • Use the fixed release date 2023-11-18.  
       • Put exactly one bullet describing the fix, verbatim as written.

       ----------  CUT HERE  ----------
       ## [0.9.5] – 2023-11-18
       - Fixed MQTT reconnection issue causing sporadic data loss on ESP32 edge nodes.
       ----------  CUT HERE  ----------

       The new section must be the **first** thing in the file (i.e. above every older section).

    Step 3 – Git bookkeeping  
       • Stage the modified files.  
       • Create a commit with the message  
         chore(release): bump version to 0.9.5  
       • Create an **annotated** tag named v0.9.5 whose tag message is exactly  
         Release 0.9.5

    Step 4 – Release log file  
       • Make the directory /home/user/edge-devices/releases if it does not exist.  
       • Create a new text file /home/user/edge-devices/releases/v0.9.5.log with precisely the following four-part structure:

         Line 1: Version: 0.9.5  
         Line 2: Date: 2023-11-18  
         Line 3: Tag: v0.9.5  
         Line 4: (blank line)  
         Line 5 and onwards: Changes:  
                           - Fixed MQTT reconnection issue causing sporadic data loss on ESP32 edge nodes.

         An example of the exact expected file content is:

         Version: 0.9.5
         Date: 2023-11-18
         Tag: v0.9.5

         Changes:
         - Fixed MQTT reconnection issue causing sporadic data loss on ESP32 edge nodes.

       • The file must end with a single newline character.

    CONSTRAINTS & HINTS
    • You do NOT need root privileges; everything happens inside /home/user.  
    • Keep to the exact wording, capitalisation, punctuation and blank lines shown above—the automated grader uses literal comparison.  
    • Remember to be inside /home/user/edge-devices (the Git repo) before running Git commands.  
    • An “annotated” tag is created with the -a flag and a message, not a lightweight tag.  
    • A typical solution will require about 7–8 shell commands (editing files + Git operations + mkdir + echo/cat).

    SUCCESS CRITERIA (checked by the grader)
    1. /home/user/edge-devices/VERSION contains exactly “0.9.5\n”.  
    2. /home/user/edge-devices/CHANGELOG.md begins with the new 0.9.5 section, exactly as shown.  
    3. A Git commit exists on branch main with message “chore(release): bump version to 0.9.5”.  
    4. Annotated tag v0.9.5 exists and points to that commit; its annotation message is “Release 0.9.5”.  
    5. /home/user/edge-devices/releases/v0.9.5.log exists and its contents match the required structure exactly.
truth:
Initial repository path: /home/user/edge-devices

    Initial files provided to the agent before the task starts:
    1. /home/user/edge-devices/VERSION
       Contents (including trailing newline):
       0.9.4\n

    2. /home/user/edge-devices/CHANGELOG.md
       Contents (including trailing newline):
       # Changelog
       
       All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
       
       The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
       and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
       
       ## [0.9.4] – 2023-10-07
       - Added support for dynamic topic subscriptions.
       
       ## [0.9.3] – 2023-09-12
       - Improved TLS handshake performance.
       
       (File ends with a newline)

    Expected files after successful completion (exact literal content):

    1. /home/user/edge-devices/VERSION
       0.9.5\n

    2. /home/user/edge-devices/CHANGELOG.md
       ## [0.9.5] – 2023-11-18
       - Fixed MQTT reconnection issue causing sporadic data loss on ESP32 edge nodes.

       # Changelog
       
       All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
       
       The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
       and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
       
       ## [0.9.4] – 2023-10-07
       - Added support for dynamic topic subscriptions.
       
       ## [0.9.3] – 2023-09-12
       - Improved TLS handshake performance.
       
       (File ends with a newline)

    3. /home/user/edge-devices/releases/v0.9.5.log
       Version: 0.9.5
       Date: 2023-11-18
       Tag: v0.9.5

       Changes:
       - Fixed MQTT reconnection issue causing sporadic data loss on ESP32 edge nodes.
       (File ends with a newline)

    Git expectations:
    • One new commit on branch main with SHA unknown, message “chore(release): bump version to 0.9.5”.  
    • Annotated tag “v0.9.5” whose tag message/body is exactly “Release 0.9.5\n”.
